description: Trigger discrimination for RANKING method selection/application (this skill) vs SEQUENCING the ranked set over time (roadmap-under-uncertainty-planner), vs specifying one item (product-spec-writer), vs eliciting the underlying need (requirements-gathering-facilitator).

cases.prompt: Pick the right prioritization framework for our backlog and apply it — surface the inputs, mark which are guesses, and rank the items honestly.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — choosing and applying a scoring frame to rank items is prioritization. No time-sequencing over horizons and no single-item spec is being produced.
cases.reason: Selecting the fitting frame and producing an honest ranking is this skill's core scope; it hands the ranked set off for time-sequencing.

cases.prompt: Items are ranked. Now sequence them into a roadmap across now/next/later with confidence and a re-plan cadence.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack, reverse) — placing an already-ranked set over horizons with confidence is roadmap planning. The ranking method is upstream and done.
cases.reason: roadmap-under-uncertainty-planner sequences and communicates over time. This skill produced the ranking it consumes.

cases.prompt: The top-ranked item is chosen. Write its product spec: problem, scope, scenarios, acceptance criteria.
cases.reason: product-spec-writer specifies one selected item. This skill ranks candidates; it does not detail any one of them.

cases.prompt: Honestly we don't know what most of these items are even worth or who needs them — we're guessing at everything.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — when the value/need is genuinely unknown, elicitation comes before ranking. Ranking unknowns is confident nonsense.
cases.reason: requirements-gathering-facilitator establishes the need and value. This skill ranks candidates whose value is at least estimable; it refuses to rank pure unknowns.
